proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients receiving treatment_enzalutamide have a higher rate of objective_response than those not receiving it.
Patients receiving treatment_abiraterone have a higher rate of objective_response than those not receiving it.
Patients receiving treatment_docetaxel have a higher rate of objective_response than those not receiving it.
Patients receiving treatment_olaparib have a higher rate of objective_response than those not receiving it.
Patients receiving treatment_lu177_psma have a higher rate of objective_response than those not receiving it.
Patients receiving treatment_pembrolizumab have a higher rate of objective_response than those not receiving it.

analyses.code:
# chi-square / two-proportion z-test on objective_response by treatment_enzalutamide
g1 = df.loc[df['treatment_enzalutamide']==1,'objective_response']
g0 = df.loc[df['treatment_enzalutamide']==0,'objective_response']
stats.chi2_contingency(pd.crosstab(df['treatment_enzalutamide'], df['objective_response']))
analyses.result_summary: treatment_enzalutamide: response 36.1% treated vs 15.9% untreated; absolute diff = +0.2024 (n_treated=20076, p=<1e-100).

analyses.code:
# chi-square / two-proportion z-test on objective_response by treatment_abiraterone
g1 = df.loc[df['treatment_abiraterone']==1,'objective_response']
g0 = df.loc[df['treatment_abiraterone']==0,'objective_response']
stats.chi2_contingency(pd.crosstab(df['treatment_abiraterone'], df['objective_response']))
analyses.result_summary: treatment_abiraterone: response 23.8% treated vs 24.1% untreated; absolute diff = -0.0031 (n_treated=14991, p=0.470).

stats.chi2_contingency(pd.crosstab(df['treatment_docetaxel'], df['objective_response']))
analyses.result_summary: treatment_docetaxel: response 24.0% treated vs 24.0% untreated; absolute diff = -0.0005 (n_treated=15187, p=0.914).

analyses.result_summary: treatment_olaparib: response 23.9% treated vs 24.0% untreated; absolute diff = -0.0010 (n_treated=5098, p=0.886).

g0 = df.loc[df['treatment_lu177_psma']==0,'objective_response']

analyses.result_summary: treatment_lu177_psma: response 24.3% treated vs 24.0% untreated; absolute diff = +0.0029 (n_treated=7504, p=0.600).

analyses.result_summary: treatment_pembrolizumab: response 23.9% treated vs 24.0% untreated; absolute diff = -0.0016 (n_treated=2384, p=0.876).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher age_years is associated with a difference in objective_response rate (continuous association).
Higher psa_ng_ml is associated with a difference in objective_response rate (continuous association).
Higher albumin_g_dl is associated with a difference in objective_response rate (continuous association).
Higher ldh_u_l is associated with a difference in objective_response rate (continuous association).
Higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with a difference in objective_response rate (continuous association).
Higher crp_mg_l is associated with a difference in objective_response rate (continuous association).
Higher nlr is associated with a difference in objective_response rate (continuous association).
Higher hemoglobin_g_dl is associated with a difference in objective_response rate (continuous association).
Higher alkaline_phosphatase_u_l is associated with a difference in objective_response rate (continuous association).

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ age_years', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Logit coef for age_years = +0.00024 (OR=1.0002 per unit increase, p=0.820); higher age_years is associated with higher odds of response.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ psa_ng_ml', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Logit coef for psa_ng_ml = -0.00468 (OR=0.9953 per unit increase, p=1.58e-94); higher psa_ng_ml is associated with lower odds of response.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ albumin_g_dl', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Logit coef for albumin_g_dl = +0.09595 (OR=1.1007 per unit increase, p=4.38e-06); higher albumin_g_dl is associated with higher odds of response.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ ldh_u_l', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Logit coef for ldh_u_l = -0.00005 (OR=0.9999 per unit increase, p=0.681); higher ldh_u_l is associated with lower odds of response.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ weight_loss_pct_6mo', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Logit coef for weight_loss_pct_6mo = -0.03147 (OR=0.9690 per unit increase, p=3.39e-29); higher weight_loss_pct_6mo is associated with lower odds of response.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ crp_mg_l', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Logit coef for crp_mg_l = -0.00690 (OR=0.9931 per unit increase, p=5.62e-08); higher crp_mg_l is associated with lower odds of response.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ nlr', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Logit coef for nlr = -0.00256 (OR=0.9974 per unit increase, p=0.612); higher nlr is associated with lower odds of response.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ hemoglobin_g_dl', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Logit coef for hemoglobin_g_dl = -0.00382 (OR=0.9962 per unit increase, p=0.513); higher hemoglobin_g_dl is associated with lower odds of response.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ alkaline_phosphatase_u_l', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Logit coef for alkaline_phosphatase_u_l = +0.00020 (OR=1.0002 per unit increase, p=0.336); higher alkaline_phosphatase_u_l is associated with higher odds of response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Patients with mcrpc=1 have a different rate of objective_response than patients with mcrpc=0.
Patients with visceral_mets=1 have a different rate of objective_response than patients with visceral_mets=0.
Patients with brca2_mutation=1 have a different rate of objective_response than patients with brca2_mutation=0.
Patients with ar_v7_positive=1 have a different rate of objective_response than patients with ar_v7_positive=0.
Patients with msi_high=1 have a different rate of objective_response than patients with msi_high=0.
Patients with psma_high=1 have a different rate of objective_response than patients with psma_high=0.

analyses.code: chi2 of objective_response by mcrpc
analyses.result_summary: Response rate 15.4% for mcrpc=1 vs 34.6% for mcrpc=0; absolute diff = -0.1925, p=<1e-100. mcrpc=1 has lower response.

analyses.code: chi2 of objective_response by visceral_mets
analyses.result_summary: Response rate 24.0% for visceral_mets=1 vs 24.0% for visceral_mets=0; absolute diff = -0.0008, p=0.871. visceral_mets=1 has lower response.

analyses.code: chi2 of objective_response by brca2_mutation
analyses.result_summary: Response rate 15.0% for brca2_mutation=1 vs 25.0% for brca2_mutation=0; absolute diff = -0.1005, p=5.43e-56. brca2_mutation=1 has lower response.

analyses.code: chi2 of objective_response by ar_v7_positive
analyses.result_summary: Response rate 16.0% for ar_v7_positive=1 vs 26.0% for ar_v7_positive=0; absolute diff = -0.1005, p=1.90e-98. ar_v7_positive=1 has lower response.

analyses.code: chi2 of objective_response by msi_high
analyses.result_summary: Response rate 17.6% for msi_high=1 vs 24.2% for msi_high=0; absolute diff = -0.0662, p=2.97e-09. msi_high=1 has lower response.

analyses.code: chi2 of objective_response by psma_high
analyses.result_summary: Response rate 23.9% for psma_high=1 vs 24.3% for psma_high=0; absolute diff = -0.0040, p=0.305. psma_high=1 has lower response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Higher ecog_ps (worse performance status) is associated with lower objective_response rate.
Patients with mcrpc=1 (castration-resistant disease) have lower objective_response rate than mcrpc=0.
Patients with visceral_mets=1 have lower objective_response rate than those without visceral metastases.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ ecog_ps', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Logit coef for ecog_ps = -0.2851 (OR per unit = 0.7519, p=9.53e-73); higher ECOG -> lower response.

analyses.code: chi2 of objective_response by mcrpc
analyses.result_summary: Response 15.4% for mcrpc=1 vs 34.6% for mcrpc=0; diff=-0.1925, p=<1e-100.

analyses.code: chi2 of objective_response by visceral_mets
analyses.result_summary: Response 24.0% for visceral_mets=1 vs 24.0% for visceral_mets=0; diff=-0.0008, p=0.871.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment effect of treatment_olaparib on objective_response is greater (more positive) in patients with brca2_mutation=1 than in patients with brca2_mutation=0 (positive treatment x BRCA2 interaction).
Within the brca2_mutation=1 subgroup, treatment_olaparib increases objective_response rate compared to no olaparib.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ treatment_olaparib*brca2_mutation', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Olaparib x BRCA2 interaction coefficient = -0.2891 (p=0.045). Treatment effect in BRCA2+: -0.0321; in BRCA2-: +0.0031. Direction of interaction is OPPOSITE of clinical prior — olaparib effect is slightly NEGATIVE in BRCA2+.

analyses.code: diff_prop on olaparib within brca2_mutation==1 subset
analyses.result_summary: Within BRCA2+ (n_olap=529): RR olap=12.1% vs no olap=15.3%; diff=-0.0321. Olaparib does NOT improve response in BRCA2+ in this dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on objective_response is greater in patients with msi_high=1 than in patients with msi_high=0 (positive treatment x MSI-high interaction).
Within the msi_high=1 subgroup, treatment_pembrolizumab increases objective_response.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ treatment_pembrolizumab*msi_high', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Pembro x MSI-high interaction coef = +0.0168 (p=0.956). TE in MSI-high: +0.0012; in MSI-stable: -0.0015. No predictive interaction observed.

analyses.code: diff_prop on pembrolizumab within msi_high==1
analyses.result_summary: Within MSI-high (n_pem=79): RR pem=17.7% vs no pem=17.6%; diff=+0.0012. Pembrolizumab does NOT improve response in MSI-high in this dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment effect of treatment_lu177_psma on objective_response is greater in patients with psma_high=1 than in patients with psma_high=0.
Within the psma_high=1 subgroup, treatment_lu177_psma increases objective_response.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ treatment_lu177_psma*psma_high', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Lu177-PSMA x PSMA-high interaction coef = -0.0541 (p=0.363). TE in PSMA-high: -0.0011; in PSMA-low: +0.0089. No predictive interaction.

analyses.code: diff_prop on lu177-psma within psma_high==1
analyses.result_summary: Within PSMA-high (n_lu=4486): RR lu=23.8% vs no lu=23.9%; diff=-0.0011. Lu177-PSMA does NOT improve response in PSMA-high in this dataset.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment effect of treatment_enzalutamide on objective_response is greater in ar_v7_positive=0 than in ar_v7_positive=1 (negative interaction, ar_v7 abrogates enzalutamide benefit).
The treatment effect of treatment_abiraterone on objective_response is greater in ar_v7_positive=0 than in ar_v7_positive=1.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ treatment_enzalutamide*ar_v7_positive', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Enzalutamide x AR-V7 interaction coef = -1.237 (p=1.39e-93). TE in AR-V7-: +0.2510 (n_enz=None); TE in AR-V7+: +0.0085. Strong AR-V7-related abrogation of enzalutamide benefit.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ treatment_abiraterone*ar_v7_positive', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Abiraterone x AR-V7 interaction coef = +0.063 (p=0.325). TE in AR-V7-: -0.0049; TE in AR-V7+: +0.0050. No meaningful interaction; abiraterone shows no effect overall.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment effect of treatment_enzalutamide is smaller in patients with mcrpc=1 than mcrpc=0 (negative enzalutamide x mcrpc interaction).
The treatment effect of treatment_enzalutamide is smaller in patients with higher ecog_ps.
Treatment effects of all six treatments differ between mcrpc=0 and mcrpc=1.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ treatment_enzalutamide*mcrpc', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Enzalutamide x mcrpc interaction coef = -1.9776 (p=<1e-100). Strong negative interaction: enzalutamide benefit is concentrated in mcrpc=0.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ treatment_enzalutamide*ecog_ps', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Enzalutamide x ecog_ps interaction coef = +0.1414 (p=1.56e-05).

analyses.code: Per-treatment logit interaction with mcrpc
analyses.result_summary: Per-treatment x mcrpc interactions: treatment_enzalutamide: coef=-1.9776, p=<1e-100; treatment_abiraterone: coef=+0.0483, p=0.311; treatment_docetaxel: coef=-0.0231, p=0.626; treatment_olaparib: coef=+0.0357, p=0.620; treatment_lu177_psma: coef=+0.0341, p=0.574; treatment_pembrolizumab: coef=+0.0912, p=0.371. Only enzalutamide shows a strong, significant interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for age_years, ecog_ps, mcrpc, visceral_mets, psa_ng_ml, albumin_g_dl, ldh_u_l, hemoglobin_g_dl, alkaline_phosphatase_u_l, gleason_score, crp_mg_l, nlr, weight_loss_pct_6mo, treatment_enzalutamide is associated with higher objective_response.

After adjusting for age_years, ecog_ps, mcrpc, visceral_mets, psa_ng_ml, albumin_g_dl, ldh_u_l, hemoglobin_g_dl, alkaline_phosphatase_u_l, gleason_score, crp_mg_l, nlr, weight_loss_pct_6mo, treatment_abiraterone is associated with higher objective_response.

After adjusting for age_years, ecog_ps, mcrpc, visceral_mets, psa_ng_ml, albumin_g_dl, ldh_u_l, hemoglobin_g_dl, alkaline_phosphatase_u_l, gleason_score, crp_mg_l, nlr, weight_loss_pct_6mo, treatment_docetaxel is associated with higher objective_response.

After adjusting for age_years, ecog_ps, mcrpc, visceral_mets, psa_ng_ml, albumin_g_dl, ldh_u_l, hemoglobin_g_dl, alkaline_phosphatase_u_l, gleason_score, crp_mg_l, nlr, weight_loss_pct_6mo, treatment_olaparib is associated with higher objective_response.

After adjusting for age_years, ecog_ps, mcrpc, visceral_mets, psa_ng_ml, albumin_g_dl, ldh_u_l, hemoglobin_g_dl, alkaline_phosphatase_u_l, gleason_score, crp_mg_l, nlr, weight_loss_pct_6mo, treatment_lu177_psma is associated with higher objective_response.

After adjusting for age_years, ecog_ps, mcrpc, visceral_mets, psa_ng_ml, albumin_g_dl, ldh_u_l, hemoglobin_g_dl, alkaline_phosphatase_u_l, gleason_score, crp_mg_l, nlr, weight_loss_pct_6mo, treatment_pembrolizumab is associated with higher objective_response.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ treatment_enzalutamide + age_years + ecog_ps + mcrpc + visceral_mets + psa_ng_ml + albumin_g_dl + ldh_u_l + hemoglobin_g_dl + alkaline_phosphatase_u_l + gleason_score + crp_mg_l + nlr + weight_loss_pct_6mo', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted logit coef for treatment_enzalutamide = +1.1779 (OR=3.2475, p=<1e-100). Adjusted: higher odds of response.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ treatment_abiraterone + age_years + ecog_ps + mcrpc + visceral_mets + psa_ng_ml + albumin_g_dl + ldh_u_l + hemoglobin_g_dl + alkaline_phosphatase_u_l + gleason_score + crp_mg_l + nlr + weight_loss_pct_6mo', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted logit coef for treatment_abiraterone = -0.0238 (OR=0.9765, p=0.315). Adjusted: lower odds of response.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ treatment_docetaxel + age_years + ecog_ps + mcrpc + visceral_mets + psa_ng_ml + albumin_g_dl + ldh_u_l + hemoglobin_g_dl + alkaline_phosphatase_u_l + gleason_score + crp_mg_l + nlr + weight_loss_pct_6mo', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted logit coef for treatment_docetaxel = +0.0007 (OR=1.0007, p=0.976). Adjusted: higher odds of response.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ treatment_olaparib + age_years + ecog_ps + mcrpc + visceral_mets + psa_ng_ml + albumin_g_dl + ldh_u_l + hemoglobin_g_dl + alkaline_phosphatase_u_l + gleason_score + crp_mg_l + nlr + weight_loss_pct_6mo', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted logit coef for treatment_olaparib = +0.0036 (OR=1.0036, p=0.920). Adjusted: higher odds of response.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ treatment_lu177_psma + age_years + ecog_ps + mcrpc + visceral_mets + psa_ng_ml + albumin_g_dl + ldh_u_l + hemoglobin_g_dl + alkaline_phosphatase_u_l + gleason_score + crp_mg_l + nlr + weight_loss_pct_6mo', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted logit coef for treatment_lu177_psma = +0.0267 (OR=1.0270, p=0.377). Adjusted: higher odds of response.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ treatment_pembrolizumab + age_years + ecog_ps + mcrpc + visceral_mets + psa_ng_ml + albumin_g_dl + ldh_u_l + hemoglobin_g_dl + alkaline_phosphatase_u_l + gleason_score + crp_mg_l + nlr + weight_loss_pct_6mo', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted logit coef for treatment_pembrolizumab = -0.0165 (OR=0.9836, p=0.745). Adjusted: lower odds of response.

proposed_hypotheses.text: In a joint multivariable logistic regression containing all six treatments and prognostic covariates, treatment_enzalutamide retains an independent positive association with objective_response.
In the same joint model, treatment_abiraterone, treatment_docetaxel, treatment_olaparib, treatment_lu177_psma, and treatment_pembrolizumab each independently increase objective_response.

analyses.code: smf.logit with all 6 treatments + adj covariates
analyses.result_summary: Enzalutamide adjusted logit coef = +1.1782 (OR=3.2484, p=<1e-100). Enzalutamide is the only treatment with a strong independent positive coefficient.

analyses.code: smf.logit joint model
analyses.result_summary: Other treatments in adjusted joint model: treatment_abiraterone: OR=0.972, p=0.241; treatment_docetaxel: OR=1.003, p=0.908; treatment_olaparib: OR=1.018, p=0.627; treatment_lu177_psma: OR=1.034, p=0.279; treatment_pembrolizumab: OR=0.994, p=0.906. None show meaningful independent positive effects.

proposed_hypotheses.text: The treatment effect of treatment_enzalutamide on objective_response decreases with increasing psa_ng_ml (negative enzalutamide x PSA interaction).
The treatment effect of treatment_enzalutamide on objective_response decreases with increasing ldh_u_l (negative enzalutamide x LDH interaction).

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ treatment_enzalutamide*psa_ng_ml', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Enzalutamide x PSA interaction coef = -0.00867 (p=1.17e-72). Higher PSA reduces enzalutamide benefit.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ treatment_enzalutamide*ldh_u_l', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Enzalutamide x LDH interaction coef = -0.00003 (p=0.910).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within BRCA2+ patients with ECOG performance status 0-1 (good PS), treatment_olaparib increases objective_response.
Within BRCA2+ patients with albumin_g_dl >= 3.5 (well-nourished), treatment_olaparib increases objective_response.
Within BRCA2+ patients with both ECOG 0-1 AND albumin >= 3.5, treatment_olaparib increases objective_response.

analyses.code: diff_prop on olaparib within brca2_pos_ecog01 subset
analyses.result_summary: Subgroup brca2_pos_ecog01: RR olap=11.5% vs no olap=16.2%; diff=-0.0470 (n_olap=442, p=0.012). Olaparib does not improve response within these refined BRCA2+ subgroups.

analyses.code: diff_prop on olaparib within brca2_pos_alb_high subset
analyses.result_summary: Subgroup brca2_pos_alb_high: RR olap=13.5% vs no olap=15.4%; diff=-0.0192 (n_olap=393, p=0.351). Olaparib does not improve response within these refined BRCA2+ subgroups.

analyses.code: diff_prop on olaparib within brca2_pos_ecog01_alb_high subset
analyses.result_summary: Subgroup brca2_pos_ecog01_alb_high: RR olap=12.5% vs no olap=16.4%; diff=-0.0386 (n_olap=327, p=0.085). Olaparib does not improve response within these refined BRCA2+ subgroups.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within MSI-high patients with ECOG 0-1, treatment_pembrolizumab increases objective_response.
Within MSI-high patients with no visceral metastases, treatment_pembrolizumab increases objective_response.
Within MSI-high patients with ECOG 0-1 AND albumin >= 3.5, treatment_pembrolizumab increases objective_response.

analyses.code: diff_prop on pembrolizumab within msi_high_ecog01 subset
analyses.result_summary: Subgroup msi_high_ecog01: RR pem=18.6% vs no pem=17.8%; diff=+0.0078 (n_pem=70, p=0.995).

analyses.code: diff_prop on pembrolizumab within msi_high_no_visceral subset
analyses.result_summary: Subgroup msi_high_no_visceral: RR pem=18.2% vs no pem=17.2%; diff=+0.0094 (n_pem=66, p=0.976).

analyses.code: diff_prop on pembrolizumab within msi_high_ecog01_alb_high subset
analyses.result_summary: Subgroup msi_high_ecog01_alb_high: RR pem=23.8% vs no pem=18.9%; diff=+0.0488 (n_pem=42, p=0.558).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within PSMA-high patients with no visceral metastases, treatment_lu177_psma increases objective_response.
Within PSMA-high patients with ECOG 0-1 AND albumin >= 3.5, treatment_lu177_psma increases objective_response.
Within PSMA-high patients with albumin >= 3.5 AND LDH < 250, treatment_lu177_psma increases objective_response.

analyses.code: diff_prop on lu177-psma within psma_high_no_visceral subset
analyses.result_summary: Subgroup psma_high_no_visceral: RR lu=24.2% vs no lu=23.8%; diff=+0.0037 (n_lu=3567, p=0.651).

analyses.code: diff_prop on lu177-psma within psma_high_ecog01_alb_high subset
analyses.result_summary: Subgroup psma_high_ecog01_alb_high: RR lu=25.3% vs no lu=25.4%; diff=-0.0005 (n_lu=2933, p=0.973).

analyses.code: diff_prop on lu177-psma within psma_high_alb_high_low_ldh subset
analyses.result_summary: Subgroup psma_high_alb_high_low_ldh: RR lu=22.6% vs no lu=24.7%; diff=-0.0211 (n_lu=2328, p=0.031).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within ar_v7_positive=0 patients, treatment_enzalutamide markedly increases objective_response (vs ar_v7_positive=1 where no benefit is seen).
Within ar_v7_positive=0 patients with no visceral metastases, treatment_enzalutamide increases objective_response.
Within ar_v7_positive=0 patients, treatment_abiraterone increases objective_response.

analyses.code: diff_prop on enzalutamide within ar_v7_positive==0 subset
analyses.result_summary: AR-V7-: RR enz=41.1% vs no enz=16.0%; diff=+0.2510 (n_enz=16057, p=<1e-100). Strong enzalutamide benefit in AR-V7-negative.

analyses.code: diff_prop on enzalutamide within ar_v7_positive==0 & visceral_mets==0
analyses.result_summary: AR-V7- AND no visceral: RR enz=41.2% vs no enz=16.0%; diff=+0.2517 (n_enz=12818, p=<1e-100).

analyses.code: diff_prop on abiraterone within ar_v7_positive==0 subset
analyses.result_summary: AR-V7-: RR abi=25.7% vs no abi=26.2%; diff=-0.0049 (n_abi=11968, p=0.308). Abiraterone shows essentially no effect even in AR-V7-negative.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within mcrpc=1 patients, treatment_docetaxel increases objective_response.
Within visceral_mets=1 patients, treatment_docetaxel increases objective_response.
Within ECOG 0-1 patients, treatment_docetaxel increases objective_response.

analyses.code: diff_prop on docetaxel within mcrpc_pos subset
analyses.result_summary: Subgroup mcrpc_pos: RR doce=15.2% vs no doce=15.4%; diff=-0.0015 (n_doce=8394, p=0.766). No clinically meaningful docetaxel benefit observed in this subgroup.

analyses.code: diff_prop on docetaxel within visceral subset
analyses.result_summary: Subgroup visceral: RR doce=24.2% vs no doce=23.8%; diff=+0.0041 (n_doce=3061, p=0.675). No clinically meaningful docetaxel benefit observed in this subgroup.

analyses.code: diff_prop on docetaxel within ecog01 subset
analyses.result_summary: Subgroup ecog01: RR doce=25.1% vs no doce=25.0%; diff=+0.0006 (n_doce=12907, p=0.911). No clinically meaningful docetaxel benefit observed in this subgroup.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There is a three-way treatment_olaparib x brca2_mutation x ecog_ps interaction on objective_response (the olaparib-by-BRCA2 effect varies with performance status).
There is a three-way treatment_pembrolizumab x msi_high x ecog_ps interaction on objective_response.
There is a three-way treatment_lu177_psma x psma_high x ecog_ps interaction on objective_response.

analyses.code: smf.logit with three-way interaction term olap x brca2 x ecog
analyses.result_summary: 3-way interaction coef (olap x brca2 x ecog) = +0.2934 (p=0.167). No significant 3-way interaction.

analyses.code: smf.logit with three-way interaction term pem x msi x ecog
analyses.result_summary: 3-way interaction coef (pem x msi x ecog) = +0.1257 (p=0.797). No significant 3-way interaction.

analyses.code: smf.logit with three-way interaction term lu x psma x ecog
analyses.result_summary: 3-way interaction coef (lu x psma x ecog) = -0.0284 (p=0.750). No significant 3-way interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: There exists at least one feature in {age_years,ecog_ps,mcrpc,visceral_mets,psa_ng_ml,gleason_score,brca2_mutation,ar_v7_positive,msi_high,psma_high,albumin_g_dl,ldh_u_l,weight_loss_pct_6mo,crp_mg_l,nlr,hemoglobin_g_dl,alkaline_phosphatase_u_l,ast_u_l,alt_u_l,total_bilirubin_mg_dl,creatinine_mg_dl,bun_mg_dl,sodium_meq_l,potassium_meq_l,calcium_mg_dl} that significantly modifies the effect of treatment_enzalutamide on objective_response (treatment x feature interaction).

There exists at least one feature in {age_years,ecog_ps,mcrpc,visceral_mets,psa_ng_ml,gleason_score,brca2_mutation,ar_v7_positive,msi_high,psma_high,albumin_g_dl,ldh_u_l,weight_loss_pct_6mo,crp_mg_l,nlr,hemoglobin_g_dl,alkaline_phosphatase_u_l,ast_u_l,alt_u_l,total_bilirubin_mg_dl,creatinine_mg_dl,bun_mg_dl,sodium_meq_l,potassium_meq_l,calcium_mg_dl} that significantly modifies the effect of treatment_abiraterone on objective_response (treatment x feature interaction).

There exists at least one feature in {age_years,ecog_ps,mcrpc,visceral_mets,psa_ng_ml,gleason_score,brca2_mutation,ar_v7_positive,msi_high,psma_high,albumin_g_dl,ldh_u_l,weight_loss_pct_6mo,crp_mg_l,nlr,hemoglobin_g_dl,alkaline_phosphatase_u_l,ast_u_l,alt_u_l,total_bilirubin_mg_dl,creatinine_mg_dl,bun_mg_dl,sodium_meq_l,potassium_meq_l,calcium_mg_dl} that significantly modifies the effect of treatment_docetaxel on objective_response (treatment x feature interaction).

There exists at least one feature in {age_years,ecog_ps,mcrpc,visceral_mets,psa_ng_ml,gleason_score,brca2_mutation,ar_v7_positive,msi_high,psma_high,albumin_g_dl,ldh_u_l,weight_loss_pct_6mo,crp_mg_l,nlr,hemoglobin_g_dl,alkaline_phosphatase_u_l,ast_u_l,alt_u_l,total_bilirubin_mg_dl,creatinine_mg_dl,bun_mg_dl,sodium_meq_l,potassium_meq_l,calcium_mg_dl} that significantly modifies the effect of treatment_olaparib on objective_response (treatment x feature interaction).

There exists at least one feature in {age_years,ecog_ps,mcrpc,visceral_mets,psa_ng_ml,gleason_score,brca2_mutation,ar_v7_positive,msi_high,psma_high,albumin_g_dl,ldh_u_l,weight_loss_pct_6mo,crp_mg_l,nlr,hemoglobin_g_dl,alkaline_phosphatase_u_l,ast_u_l,alt_u_l,total_bilirubin_mg_dl,creatinine_mg_dl,bun_mg_dl,sodium_meq_l,potassium_meq_l,calcium_mg_dl} that significantly modifies the effect of treatment_lu177_psma on objective_response (treatment x feature interaction).

There exists at least one feature in {age_years,ecog_ps,mcrpc,visceral_mets,psa_ng_ml,gleason_score,brca2_mutation,ar_v7_positive,msi_high,psma_high,albumin_g_dl,ldh_u_l,weight_loss_pct_6mo,crp_mg_l,nlr,hemoglobin_g_dl,alkaline_phosphatase_u_l,ast_u_l,alt_u_l,total_bilirubin_mg_dl,creatinine_mg_dl,bun_mg_dl,sodium_meq_l,potassium_meq_l,calcium_mg_dl} that significantly modifies the effect of treatment_pembrolizumab on objective_response (treatment x feature interaction).

analyses.code: For each feature f: smf.logit('objective_response ~ treatment_enzalutamide*f', data=df).fit() -- record interaction p-value
analyses.result_summary: Top 5 strongest interactions for treatment_enzalutamide: mcrpc: coef=-1.9776, p=<1e-100; ar_v7_positive: coef=-1.2371, p=1.39e-93; psa_ng_ml: coef=-0.0087, p=1.17e-72; brca2_mutation: coef=-1.1378, p=3.32e-42; msi_high: coef=-1.2826, p=1.76e-19.  Strongest is mcrpc (p=<1e-100).

analyses.code: For each feature f: smf.logit('objective_response ~ treatment_abiraterone*f', data=df).fit() -- record interaction p-value
analyses.result_summary: Top 5 strongest interactions for treatment_abiraterone: brca2_mutation: coef=+0.1892, p=0.031; psma_high: coef=-0.0720, p=0.122; creatinine_mg_dl: coef=+0.1080, p=0.160; bun_mg_dl: coef=-0.0061, p=0.184; crp_mg_l: coef=+0.0028, p=0.302.  Strongest is brca2_mutation (p=0.031).

analyses.code: For each feature f: smf.logit('objective_response ~ treatment_docetaxel*f', data=df).fit() -- record interaction p-value
analyses.result_summary: Top 5 strongest interactions for treatment_docetaxel: sodium_meq_l: coef=-0.0171, p=0.023; alt_u_l: coef=-0.0042, p=0.028; alkaline_phosphatase_u_l: coef=+0.0009, p=0.060; total_bilirubin_mg_dl: coef=+0.1194, p=0.097; albumin_g_dl: coef=+0.0663, p=0.145.  Strongest is sodium_meq_l (p=0.023).

analyses.code: For each feature f: smf.logit('objective_response ~ treatment_olaparib*f', data=df).fit() -- record interaction p-value
analyses.result_summary: Top 5 strongest interactions for treatment_olaparib: bun_mg_dl: coef=-0.0151, p=0.031; brca2_mutation: coef=-0.2891, p=0.045; sodium_meq_l: coef=+0.0222, p=0.058; potassium_meq_l: coef=-0.1171, p=0.087; age_years: coef=+0.0051, p=0.142.  Strongest is bun_mg_dl (p=0.031).

analyses.code: For each feature f: smf.logit('objective_response ~ treatment_lu177_psma*f', data=df).fit() -- record interaction p-value
analyses.result_summary: Top 5 strongest interactions for treatment_lu177_psma: visceral_mets: coef=-0.1905, p=0.010; creatinine_mg_dl: coef=+0.1940, p=0.046; gleason_score: coef=+0.0564, p=0.050; crp_mg_l: coef=-0.0050, p=0.178; brca2_mutation: coef=+0.1424, p=0.206.  Strongest is visceral_mets (p=0.010).

analyses.code: For each feature f: smf.logit('objective_response ~ treatment_pembrolizumab*f', data=df).fit() -- record interaction p-value
analyses.result_summary: Top 5 strongest interactions for treatment_pembrolizumab: gleason_score: coef=+0.1045, p=0.033; crp_mg_l: coef=-0.0140, p=0.061; albumin_g_dl: coef=+0.1798, p=0.071; ecog_ps: coef=-0.1370, p=0.074; visceral_mets: coef=-0.2071, p=0.099.  Strongest is gleason_score (p=0.033).

proposed_hypotheses.text: In the subgroup defined by (ar_v7_positive=0,mcrpc=0), treatment_enzalutamide produces a markedly higher objective_response rate than no treatment.
In the subgroup defined by (psma_high=1,visceral_mets=1), treatment_olaparib produces a markedly higher objective_response rate than no treatment.
In the subgroup defined by (msi_high=1,mcrpc=0), treatment_pembrolizumab produces a markedly higher objective_response rate than no treatment.
In the subgroup defined by (msi_high=1,ar_v7_positive=1), treatment_lu177_psma produces a markedly higher objective_response rate than no treatment.

analyses.code: For each pair (f1,f2) of binary features and each direction combo, compute treatment effect of treatment_enzalutamide within subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Best two-feature subgroup for treatment_enzalutamide: (ar_v7_positive=0,mcrpc=0) with RR_t=71.7% vs RR_not=17.1%, diff=+0.5459 (p=<1e-100, n_t=7246, n_not=10754).

analyses.code: For each pair (f1,f2) of binary features and each direction combo, compute treatment effect of treatment_olaparib within subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Best two-feature subgroup for treatment_olaparib: (psma_high=1,visceral_mets=1) with RR_t=25.8% vs RR_not=23.7%, diff=+0.0209 (p=0.282, n_t=586, n_not=5435).

analyses.code: For each pair (f1,f2) of binary features and each direction combo, compute treatment effect of treatment_pembrolizumab within subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Best two-feature subgroup for treatment_pembrolizumab: (msi_high=1,mcrpc=0) with RR_t=29.4% vs RR_not=19.4%, diff=+0.1002 (p=0.227, n_t=34, n_not=655).

analyses.code: For each pair (f1,f2) of binary features and each direction combo, compute treatment effect of treatment_lu177_psma within subgroup
analyses.result_summary: Best two-feature subgroup for treatment_lu177_psma: (msi_high=1,ar_v7_positive=1) with RR_t=25.5% vs RR_not=17.3%, diff=+0.0822 (p=0.258, n_t=47, n_not=260).

proposed_hypotheses.text: Across {brca2_mutation, mcrpc, visceral_mets} 3-way subgroups, treatment_olaparib's largest treatment effect on objective_response defines its responder subgroup.
Across {msi_high, mcrpc, visceral_mets} 3-way subgroups, treatment_pembrolizumab's largest treatment effect defines its responder subgroup.
Across {psma_high, mcrpc, visceral_mets} 3-way subgroups, treatment_lu177_psma's largest treatment effect defines its responder subgroup.

analyses.code: Enumerate all 8 combinations of three binary features and compute treatment effect of treatment_olaparib
analyses.result_summary: Best 3-way subgroup for treatment_olaparib: (brca2_mutation=0,mcrpc=1,visceral_mets=0) RR_t=16.2% vs RR_not=15.3%, diff=+0.0085 (p=0.329, n_t=2023). Even the best 3-way subgroup shows a small, non-clinically-meaningful effect for these biomarker-defined treatments.

analyses.code: Enumerate all 8 combinations of three binary features and compute treatment effect of treatment_pembrolizumab
analyses.result_summary: Best 3-way subgroup for treatment_pembrolizumab: (msi_high=1,mcrpc=0,visceral_mets=0) RR_t=30.8% vs RR_not=19.2%, diff=+0.1154 (p=0.234, n_t=26). Even the best 3-way subgroup shows a small, non-clinically-meaningful effect for these biomarker-defined treatments.

analyses.code: Enumerate all 8 combinations of three binary features and compute treatment effect of treatment_lu177_psma
analyses.result_summary: Best 3-way subgroup for treatment_lu177_psma: (psma_high=0,mcrpc=0,visceral_mets=0) RR_t=38.1% vs RR_not=34.4%, diff=+0.0370 (p=0.021, n_t=1066). Even the best 3-way subgroup shows a small, non-clinically-meaningful effect for these biomarker-defined treatments.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within BRCA2+ patients, ECOG performance status, mcrpc, visceral_mets, or ar_v7_positive modify the (already-null) treatment effect of treatment_olaparib.
Within MSI-high patients, ECOG, mcrpc, visceral_mets, or ar_v7_positive modify the treatment effect of treatment_pembrolizumab.
Within PSMA-high patients, ECOG, mcrpc, visceral_mets, or ar_v7_positive modify the treatment effect of treatment_lu177_psma.

analyses.code: Within biomarker-positive subgroup, stratify by candidate modifier and recompute treatment_olaparib effect
analyses.result_summary: Most-favorable strata for treatment_olaparib: ecog_ps=high -> diff=+0.0474, p=0.246; least-favorable: ecog_ps=low -> diff=-0.0443, p=0.181. No modifier converts the null effect into a meaningful benefit.

analyses.code: Within biomarker-positive subgroup, stratify by candidate modifier and recompute treatment_pembrolizumab effect
analyses.result_summary: Most-favorable strata for treatment_pembrolizumab: mcrpc=low -> diff=+0.1002, p=0.227; least-favorable: mcrpc=high -> diff=-0.0723, p=0.278. No modifier converts the null effect into a meaningful benefit.

analyses.code: Within biomarker-positive subgroup, stratify by candidate modifier and recompute treatment_lu177_psma effect
analyses.result_summary: Most-favorable strata for treatment_lu177_psma: mcrpc=high -> diff=+0.0092, p=0.254; least-favorable: ecog_ps=high -> diff=-0.0223, p=0.184. No modifier converts the null effect into a meaningful benefit.

proposed_hypotheses.text: After adjusting for ecog_ps, visceral_mets, albumin_g_dl, ldh_u_l, age_years, and mcrpc, the treatment_olaparib x brca2_mutation interaction on objective_response is positive (BRCA2+ predicts olaparib benefit).
After adjusting for the same prognostic covariates, the treatment_pembrolizumab x msi_high interaction is positive.
After adjusting for the same prognostic covariates, the treatment_lu177_psma x psma_high interaction is positive.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ treatment_olaparib*brca2_mutation + ecog_ps + visceral_mets + albumin_g_dl + ldh_u_l + age_years + mcrpc', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted olaparib x BRCA2 interaction coef = -0.2765 (p=0.060). The interaction is NEGATIVE — direction is OPPOSITE of the canonical clinical hypothesis.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ treatment_pembrolizumab*msi_high + adj_covs', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted pem x MSI interaction coef = +0.0730 (p=0.816). No predictive interaction.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ treatment_lu177_psma*psma_high + adj_covs', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Adjusted lu x PSMA interaction coef = -0.0650 (p=0.289). No predictive interaction.

proposed_hypotheses.text: Within BRCA2+ patients with ECOG=0, treatment_olaparib increases objective_response.
Within MSI-high patients with ECOG=0, treatment_pembrolizumab increases objective_response.
Within PSMA-high patients with ECOG=0, treatment_lu177_psma increases objective_response.

analyses.code: diff_prop within biomarker+ AND ecog_ps==0
analyses.result_summary: Subgroup olap_brca2_ecog0: RR_t=15.3% vs RR_not=19.7%, diff=-0.0443 (n_t=183, p=0.181). Even restricted to good performance status, no benefit emerges.

analyses.code: diff_prop within biomarker+ AND ecog_ps==0
analyses.result_summary: Subgroup pem_msi_ecog0: RR_t=20.7% vs RR_not=22.3%, diff=-0.0166 (n_t=29, p=1.000). Even restricted to good performance status, no benefit emerges.

analyses.code: diff_prop within biomarker+ AND ecog_ps==0
analyses.result_summary: Subgroup lu_psma_ecog0: RR_t=27.4% vs RR_not=28.0%, diff=-0.0052 (n_t=1607, p=0.693). Even restricted to good performance status, no benefit emerges.

proposed_hypotheses.text: FINAL: Within the subgroup defined by mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7_positive=0 AND brca2_mutation=0 AND msi_high=0, treatment_enzalutamide produces a large increase in objective_response (markedly higher than untreated patients in the same subgroup).

FINAL: The treatment effect of treatment_enzalutamide on objective_response is suppressed (driven to zero) by ANY of the unfavorable covariate values mcrpc=1, ar_v7_positive=1, brca2_mutation=1, or msi_high=1; the strongest single suppressor is mcrpc=1.

FINAL: There is no biomarker-defined subgroup in this dataset in which treatment_olaparib increases objective_response among brca2_mutation=1 patients (in fact, the point estimate is slightly negative).
FINAL: There is no biomarker-defined subgroup in this dataset in which treatment_pembrolizumab increases objective_response among msi_high=1 patients.
FINAL: There is no biomarker-defined subgroup in this dataset in which treatment_lu177_psma increases objective_response among psma_high=1 patients.
FINAL: treatment_abiraterone and treatment_docetaxel show no main effect and no biomarker-defined subgroup with meaningful benefit on objective_response in this cohort.

analyses.code: Within mcrpc==0 & ar_v7_positive==0 & brca2_mutation==0 & msi_high==0: chi2 of objective_response by treatment_enzalutamide
analyses.result_summary: Final responder subgroup (mcrpc=0 AND ar_v7=0 AND brca2=0 AND msi=0, n=15681): RR enz=79.8% vs no enz=17.2%; absolute diff = +0.6257 (n_enz=6325, n_no_enz=9356, p=<1e-100). This is the best-supported predictive subgroup in the dataset.

analyses.code: smf.logit('objective_response ~ treatment_enzalutamide*(mcrpc + ar_v7_positive + brca2_mutation + msi_high) + ecog_ps + visceral_mets + albumin_g_dl + ldh_u_l + age_years', data=df).fit()
analyses.result_summary: Joint adjusted model of enzalutamide effect modification: main enz coef = +2.611 (p=<1e-100); enz x mcrpc = -2.222 (p=<1e-100); enz x ar_v7_positive = -1.634 (p=<1e-100); enz x brca2_mutation = -1.554 (p=2.85e-67); enz x msi_high = -1.665 (p=1.48e-27). All four interactions are large, negative, and highly significant. mcrpc has the largest negative interaction coefficient (-2.22), making it the dominant single suppressor.

analyses.code: Within brca2_mutation==1: chi2 + OR for treatment_olaparib on objective_response
analyses.result_summary: Olaparib in BRCA2+ (n_t=529, n_not=4467): OR=0.761 (95% CI 0.579-1.001); RR diff=-0.0321; p=0.058. Direction is slightly negative, fails to reject null.

analyses.code: Within msi_high==1: chi2 + OR for treatment_pembrolizumab on objective_response
analyses.result_summary: Pembrolizumab in MSI-high (n_t=79, n_not=1449): OR=1.009 (95% CI 0.557-1.825); RR diff=+0.0012; p=1.000. Effectively null.

analyses.code: Within psma_high==1: chi2 + OR for treatment_lu177_psma on objective_response
Marginal and adjusted main effects of treatment_abiraterone and treatment_docetaxel on objective_response
analyses.result_summary: Abiraterone marginal diff=-0.0031 (p=0.470); docetaxel marginal diff=-0.0005 (p=0.914). Adjusted ORs (~1.0). Treatment-by-feature screens (iteration 19) yield no biologically credible predictive interaction either. No subgroup defined by the available features identifies responders to these two drugs in this dataset.
